summary: Senior Java Backend Engineer with 5 plus years of experience designing and building high-performance, distributed backend systems. Expert in Spring Boot 3 with Java 17 and 21, microservices architecture, event-driven systems with Apache Kafka, and cloud-native deployment on GCP and AWS. Strong emphasis on code quality, test coverage, and production reliability.
keyStrengths: Java expert: Java 8 through 21 — streams, CompletableFuture, virtual threads (Project Loom), records, pattern matching
Spring Boot 3: REST APIs, WebSocket, Spring Security, Spring Data JPA, Spring Kafka, Spring Actuator
Microservices: service decomposition, API gateway patterns, circuit breakers, distributed tracing
Apache Kafka: high-throughput event streaming, partitioning, consumer groups, exactly-once semantics, DLQ patterns
Database expertise: PostgreSQL advanced queries, index tuning, connection pooling (HikariCP), transaction management
API design: RESTful principles, versioning, rate limiting, pagination, error handling standards
Concurrency: virtual threads (Java 21), CompletableFuture, ExecutorService, ConcurrentHashMap, AtomicReference
Testing: JUnit 5, Mockito, Testcontainers, integration tests, 90 percent plus coverage

Implemented WebSocket server for real-time audio streaming (Twilio Media Streams integration) — non-blocking audio pipeline processing concurrent calls with Java virtual threads
Integrated external AI APIs (Vertex AI, Gemini, Claude, Deepgram, ElevenLabs) with resilience patterns: retries with exponential backoff, circuit breakers, timeout management
Built Spring Security configurations: JWT validation, role-based access control, CORS policies, rate limiting
Optimized PostgreSQL queries: added composite indexes, rewrote N+1 queries using JOIN FETCH, implemented read replicas for reporting workloads
Deployed Spring Boot services on GCP Cloud Run: Docker multi-stage builds, health check tuning, graceful shutdown, environment-based configuration
Built comprehensive test suites: JUnit 5 unit tests, Mockito-based service layer tests, Testcontainers integration tests against real Postgres and Kafka — 90 percent plus coverage

technicalQA.whyVirtualThreads: Java 21 virtual threads (Project Loom) solve the problem of thread-per-request overhead. Traditional platform threads are OS-level and expensive — a thread pool of 200 threads caps your concurrency at 200. Virtual threads are lightweight JVM-managed, so you can have millions. I use them at Frontier for WebSocket handling and API calls to external AI services — each blocking I/O call yields the virtual thread instead of blocking an OS thread.
technicalQA.kafkaVsRabbitMQ: Kafka is append-only log storage with replay capability — you can re-consume events from any offset. RabbitMQ is a message queue optimized for routing and fan-out. I use Kafka when I need durable, high-throughput event streaming where consumers need to process at their own pace and I might need to replay history. RabbitMQ when I need flexible routing logic or task queue semantics.
technicalQA.microservicesVsMonolith: Start monolith, extract services when you have clear bounded contexts and scaling requirements. Microservices add operational complexity — service discovery, distributed tracing, network latency, eventual consistency. I have built both. At Frontier we run microservices because different teams own different domains and need independent deployment. The AI pipeline runs as its own service so it can scale independently from the customer portal.
technicalQA.databaseIndexing: Index the columns you query by most, but too many indexes slow down writes. For PostgreSQL: B-tree indexes for equality and range queries, GIN indexes for JSON and full-text. Composite indexes for multi-column WHERE clauses — put the most selective column first. Run EXPLAIN ANALYZE to verify the planner actually uses your index.
